agents.name: Alert Correlator
agents.desc: Ingests Prometheus and Blackbox Exporter alerts — correlates noise into single actionable incidents. Noise reduction: 72%.

anatomy.role: First responder — ingests all monitoring signals and reduces noise to actionable incidents
tasks: Receive alert payload from Prometheus Alertmanager
Check suppression rules — is CRMURLDown already firing?
Query active_alerts dict for related signals
Apply correlation rules — group by service and failure pattern
Determine routing: autonomous (risk < 25) or HiTL (risk 25-74)
Create Zammad ticket and post Grafana annotation
anatomy.output: Single correlated Zammad ticket with all related alerts linked — RCA Engine triggered automatically
anatomy.decisionCriteria: Suppression rules checked first. Risk score determines routing: autonomous < 25, HiTL 25-74, escalate > 74
anatomy.memoryType: Stateless — active_alerts dict queried per run in RCA service
anatomy.llmReasoning: LLM builds context from Prometheus metrics, Loki logs and Blackbox probe results to form incident hypothesis
contextUsed: Prometheus alert payload — alertname, labels, annotations
active_alerts dict — currently firing alerts
Suppression rules — CRMURLDown suppresses CRMSlowResponse
Blackbox Exporter probe result — HTTP status and latency
Zammad open ticket list

agents.name: Log Analyser
agents.desc: Queries Loki for application and infrastructure logs — identifies error patterns and anomalies across demo-api, demo-worker and Odoo CRM.

anatomy.role: Evidence gatherer — queries Loki logs to build diagnostic evidence for RCA Engine
tasks: Query Loki for logs from affected service — last 30 minutes
Filter by error level — extract ERROR and WARN entries
Identify error patterns — frequency, timing, correlation with alert
Cross-reference with Prometheus metrics timeline
Rank hypotheses by log evidence strength
Pass log evidence package to RCA Engine
anatomy.output: Log evidence package with top error patterns — passed to RCA Engine for root cause confirmation
anatomy.decisionCriteria: Error frequency above baseline and timing correlation with alert onset — minimum 80% confidence before passing to RCA Engine
anatomy.memoryType: Stateless — Loki queried fresh per incident
anatomy.llmReasoning: LLM analyses log patterns to distinguish symptom logs from root cause logs — identifies the first error in the causal chain
contextUsed: Loki log stream — last 30 minutes for affected service
Prometheus metrics timeline — correlated with log timestamps
Container exit codes and restart events
Promtail label set — service, job, container name

agents.name: RCA Engine
agents.desc: Core diagnostic agent — queries Prometheus, Loki and Redis metrics to identify root cause with confidence score. Routes to autonomous or HiTL based on risk score.

anatomy.role: Diagnostic core — identifies root cause with confidence score and routes to autonomous or HiTL execution
tasks: Build context from Prometheus metrics — last 30 min for affected service
Query Loki for error logs correlated with alert onset
Check Redis queue depth — is worker consuming?
Check Docker container status via Bridge API
Build LLM prompt with all telemetry evidence
Generate RCA: root cause, confidence score, risk score
Route: risk < 25 autonomous, risk 25-74 HiTL Slack, risk > 74 escalate
anatomy.output: Structured RCA with confidence score and risk score. Autonomous: Remediation Agent executes immediately. HiTL: Slack message with Approve/Reject. Escalate: on-call paged.
anatomy.decisionCriteria: CRMURLDown risk 18 — autonomous. CRMSlowResponse risk 42 — HiTL. HighQueueDepth risk 12 — autonomous. HighErrorRate risk 45 — HiTL
anatomy.memoryType: Contextual — Prometheus and Loki queried per incident. active_alerts dict maintained in memory
anatomy.llmReasoning: Ollama gpt-oss:20b running locally on host machine via Docker internal network. Receives Prometheus metrics, Loki logs and Blackbox probe results — builds causal chain and generates root cause hypothesis with confidence score. Local inference means zero latency to external API and no data leaving the environment.
contextUsed: Prometheus metrics — request_rate, error_rate, p95_latency, queue_depth
Loki logs — error messages, container exit events, stack traces
Redis queue depth — orders pending vs worker consuming
Blackbox Exporter — HTTP probe status for demo-api and Odoo
Bridge API — api_health and crm_health live values
active_alerts dict — currently firing alerts for suppression logic

agents.name: Remediation Agent
agents.desc: Executes approved remediation actions — Docker container restarts, queue worker recovery, Slack HiTL notifications. Validates recovery via Bridge API before closing Zammad ticket.

anatomy.role: Executor — carries out approved remediation actions via Docker API, Slack HiTL and Zammad lifecycle
tasks: Receive remediation plan from RCA Engine
For autonomous: execute immediately via Docker TCP API
For HiTL: send Slack webhook with Approve/Reject buttons
On approval: execute Docker restart or worker start command
Poll Bridge API — wait for api_health or crm_health = 1
Post Grafana annotation with resolution timestamp
Update and close Zammad ticket with MTTR
anatomy.output: Container restarted and healthy. Bridge API confirms recovery. Zammad ticket closed with MTTR. Grafana annotation posted.
anatomy.decisionCriteria: Autonomous if risk < 25 and alert in AUTONOMOUS_OVERRIDES list. HiTL if risk 25-74 and alert in HITL_OVERRIDES list. Token validation required for HiTL approval
anatomy.memoryType: Stateless — remediation plan received per execution from RCA Engine
anatomy.llmReasoning: Not LLM-driven for execution — deterministic Docker API calls. LLM used only for Slack message composition and Zammad ticket summary
contextUsed: Approved remediation plan — alert name, container target, action
Bridge API — api_health and crm_health for recovery validation
Slack webhook URL — for HiTL notification
HiTL token — generated per incident for Slack approval matching
Zammad ticket ID — for update and closure

agents.name: Change Validator
agents.desc: Validates every remediation action against risk thresholds before execution. Posts Grafana annotations as the audit trail for every ZeroOps action.

anatomy.role: Gatekeeper — validates every action against risk thresholds before execution and posts audit trail
tasks: Evaluate risk score from RCA Engine output
Check alert against AUTONOMOUS_OVERRIDES and HITL_OVERRIDES lists
Validate target container is in expected state
Approve autonomous (risk < 25) or route to HiTL (risk 25-74)
Post Grafana annotation — action taken, timestamp, outcome
Log to Zammad ticket audit trail
anatomy.output: Approved with Grafana annotation posted. All actions logged. Rejected actions escalated with reason.
anatomy.decisionCriteria: AUTONOMOUS_OVERRIDES: CRMURLDown (18), HighQueueDepth (12), ServiceURLDown (18). HITL_OVERRIDES: CRMSlowResponse (42), HighErrorRate (45), HighLatency (38)
anatomy.memoryType: Stateless — risk thresholds and override lists configured in RCA service
anatomy.llmReasoning: Not LLM-driven — deterministic rules engine matching alert name against override lists and risk score thresholds
contextUsed: Alert name — matched against AUTONOMOUS_OVERRIDES and HITL_OVERRIDES
Risk score from RCA Engine — 0-100
Grafana API credentials — for annotation posting
Zammad ticket ID — for audit trail update
